question: Does the paper report HIV sequences from patient samples?
explain: The paper focuses on in vitro resistance selection studies using HIV-1 subtype A, B, and C viruses in cell culture systems. There is no mention of patient-derived samples or sequences.
sentences: "In vitro resistance selection of subtype B virus (MT4-green fluorescent protein [GFP] cells), as well as subtype A and C viruses (MT4-GFP/CCR5 cells) was conducted with DOR, rilpivirine (RPV), and efavirine (EFV) under low-multiplicity-of-infection conditions in a 96-well format."; "To evaluate potential mutations that would be selected by DOR in vitro with HIV-1 subtype A, B, and C viruses, resistance selection experiments were conducted in a 96-well format using MT4-GFP cells (for subtype B virus) with the CCR5 coreceptor (for subtype A and C viruses), which is controlled by transcription of the LTR to express GFP."

question: Does the paper report in vitro drug susceptibility data?
explain: The paper includes data on the susceptibility of mutant viruses selected by DOR, RPV, and EFV to NNRTIs. It discusses the fold resistance of various mutants to these drugs.
sentences: "The susceptibility of subtype B mutant viruses selected by DOR, RPV, and EFV to NNRTIs was evaluated."; "As shown in Fig. 3A, the V106A mutant displayed an approximately 10-fold resistance to DOR."; "In addition, the double substitutions V106A/F227L and V106A/L234I and the triple substitution V106A/L234I/V108I conferred >150-fold resistance to DOR."; "The susceptibilities of mutants selected by EFV to NNRTIs are shown in Fig. 3B."; "The L100I and K103N mutants displayed 10- and 56-fold resistance to EFV, respectively."

question: Does the paper report GenBank accession numbers for sequenced HIV isolates? If "Yes", what were the GenBank accession numbers for sequenced HIV isolates?
explain: The paper does not mention any GenBank accession numbers for sequenced HIV isolates. It focuses on in vitro resistance selection and does not provide specific accession numbers for any sequences.
